message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #2 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #2 (CSS gaps) committed, pushed, and CI GREEN (2b5789a). 

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Changes: static/css/main.css — 7 items fixed:
1. .nav-logout — margin-inline-start: auto (right-aligned in header)
2. .nav-logout__button — styled as muted text link
3. .todo-add__group — spacing below input group
4. .todo-add__submit — fixed class name mismatch (was .todo-add__button)
5. .todo-add__error + :empty — danger color, small font, collapses when empty
6. .auth-form__message:empty — collapses empty error container
7. .auth-form__actions + .auth-form__cancel — flexbox layout, cancel as text-link

This addresses all of Steve Schoger's flagged CSS gaps.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the CSS changes for Task #2.
Now let me also cross-reference the templates to verify the CSS class names match up properly, since one of the changes was fixing a class name mismatch.

content.text: Good. I've verified all the CSS classes against the templates. Let me send my review.

input.content:
**Task #2 Review -- Heydon Pickering (Accessibility)** I consent. This is a CSS-only change, so the HTML semantics and ARIA attributes are unchanged. But CSS affects accessibility in important ways, so here's my analysis: 1. **`.auth-form__message:empty { display: none; }`** -- This is the right approach. The login and edit_todo templates have `role="alert"` on the error `
`. When the element is empty (no error), `display: none` removes it from the accessibility tree entirely. This prevents screen readers from announcing an empty alert region, which would be confusing. When an error IS present, the element has content, so `:empty` doesn't match, and it becomes visible and announced. Clean. 2. **`.todo-add__error:empty { display: none; }`** -- Same pattern for the todo add error. The `

` with `role="alert"` and `id="add-todo-error"` collapses when empty. The `aria-describedby="add-todo-error"` on the input still points to this element -- when it's `display: none`, the description simply resolves to nothing, which is correct behavior. When an error appears, the element gets content, `:empty` no longer applies, and the alert is announced. Good. 3. **`.nav-logout` and `.nav-logout__button`** -- The logout is a real `
input.summary: Task #2 a11y review: I consent, no concerns

content.text: Consent sent for Task #2. Standing by for Tasks #3-5, which are Carson's domain -- HTMX focus management, empty state transitions, and inline editing. Those will be the ones that really need my attention from an accessibility perspective, particularly focus management after content swaps.
